workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Pulling in context from Northwind Traders's meeting transcript -- need to prep a brief for the escalation triage.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-01-23
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Northwind Traders)

[00:02:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:09:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:12:00] David (Northwind Traders): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] David (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:18:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:22:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-B trap (Confuses CSA disagreement with DSSP as consensus); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-B failure mode (Confuses CSA disagreement with DSSP as consensus)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-B -- Confuses CSA disagreement with DSSP as consensus
